forecasts.reasoning: Trump confirmed he will visit China after June 5 phone call with Xi, but no specific date announced as of June 9. Trade teams meeting in London June 9 suggests visit is in early planning stages. Presidential visits normally require 4-6 weeks minimum for security/protocol arrangements. With only 21-22 days remaining, and no mid-June scheduling announcements found, a visit before July 1 is unlikely but not impossible if it was fast-tracked as urgent diplomatic priority. Trump's presidential status in 2025 appears to be confirmed (won 2024 election per multiple sources), so visit is possible if scheduled. However, the combination of: (1) timeline constraints (3 weeks), (2) early planning stages (team meeting June 9), and (3) no announced date by mid-June all point to low probability. The 45% estimate reflects that while Trump accepted invitation and appears to be president, the logistical reality makes the timeline extremely tight. Evidence for: Trump publicly accepted invitation (search_0_summary, search_3_summary). Evidence against: trade teams meeting June 9 in London indicates visit not imminent, no date announced despite searches across multiple windows, typical presidential visit planning requires 4-6 weeks.
